baseline.title: The repository already contains a standalone CLI entrypoint and interactive TUI loop in src/parun.nim.
Package search data already uses a hot/cold SoA layout through PackageSOA, PackageHot, and PackageCold.
AUR and Nimble metadata already have an on-disk binary cache path and a memory-mapped reader in src/pkgs/cache.nim.
The current product contract already includes instant source switching through aur/, nim/, nimble/, and installed/ search prefixes.

current_focus.why: The roadmap is complete; focus is anchored on the non-terminal query-primitives task that feeds the finished indexed runtime.

mission: Make parun an instant multi-source package search TUI with parui-class idle memory by replacing heap-resident package state with validated, low-residency indexed storage while preserving zero visible latency for source switching.

exit_criteria: The repository can reproduce current memory and latency baselines on demand.
The dominant owners of current Private_Dirty are measured and ranked.
The Nim ownership contract for future indexed storage is explicit enough to constrain the next architecture phase.
phases.goal: Produce a repeatable memory and latency baseline, plus a precise accounting of which runtime structures currently own the app's heap footprint.

acceptance_criteria: One documented command produces baseline measurements for idle RSS, idle PSS, idle Private_Dirty, and source-switch timing on a release build.
The harness measures the real parun process rather than a wrapper shell and records the exact executable path and pid it sampled.
The measurement flow supports at least idle startup and a scripted first aur/ switch without requiring manual keystrokes.
evidence_required: Recorded measurement environment including Nim version and build mode
tasks.summary: Create a repo-local harness that builds parun in release mode, launches the real TUI under a controlled PTY, captures idle and post-switch memory from procfs, and records first-switch latency for local, AUR, and Nimble workloads.
tasks.title: Add repeatable release-build measurement harness

acceptance_criteria: The report distinguishes top-level object size from dynamically owned bytes in seq, string, and table-backed structures.
The report identifies the dominant contributors to current Private_Dirty for system, AUR, and Nimble data separately.
The report is generated without requiring the interactive UI loop to be driven manually.
evidence_required: Human-readable summary of top memory owners
Exact load scenario used to produce the report
tasks.summary: Add a non-interactive diagnostics path that loads the same package data as the app and reports compile-time sizes plus runtime reserved bytes for AppState, PackageDB instances, arenas, result buffers, repo tables, details caches, and worker-side caches.
tasks.title: Attribute heap ownership across AppState and worker structures

acceptance_criteria: The roadmap slice records the selected Nim memory-manager assumptions and how diagnostics are compiled and run.
The codebase contains a single diagnostic-oriented place where mapped-data access rules and permitted copy boundaries are defined.
The contract names which existing string-producing helpers cannot remain on the future hot path without redesign.
evidence_required: Referenced code locations for allowed and forbidden copy boundaries
tasks.summary: Document and enforce the ARC/ORC rules for future mmap-backed indexes so hot-path readers do not accidentally promote mapped slices into heap strings or widen mutable ownership beyond what the low-RAM design allows.
tasks.title: Make the Nim ownership and borrowing contract explicit for indexed data

phases.title: Establish Measured Baselines

exit_criteria: Each source has a defined immutable index format and deterministic builder.
The hot search primitive is selected with explicit memory and latency evidence.
The architecture is ready to replace heap-resident package databases without losing search coverage.
phases.goal: Replace heap-resident package corpora with immutable, file-backed source indexes whose hot access patterns are explicit and measurable.

acceptance_criteria: The index format names every section needed by the hot search path and every section reserved for cold details.
The format includes versioning and validation rules strong enough to reject corrupt or stale indexes.
The format makes explicit which sections may be prefaulted or madvise'd without forcing full-corpus residency.
tasks.summary: Define the file layout, headers, section offsets, versioning, and invariants for local, AUR, and Nimble indexes so names, normalized keys, posting lists, and cold details can be addressed without heap reconstruction.
tasks.title: Specify the binary layout for immutable source indexes

acceptance_criteria: The builder produces reproducible indexes for each source from the same input snapshot.
Generated indexes include integrity metadata that can be checked before runtime mapping.
Builder output preserves the package count and visible search corpus of the current caches.
tasks.summary: Build offline index-generation tooling that converts current package metadata into immutable source indexes with deterministic output and integrity checks.
tasks.title: Implement deterministic index builders for system, AUR, and Nimble

acceptance_criteria: The chosen primitive set has a measured byte budget and a documented reason it fits the instant-switch requirement.
Candidate narrowing avoids materializing full-name strings on the heap for ordinary typing paths.
The prototype can be evaluated against the baseline harness from phase-1.
tasks.summary: Select and prototype the compact prefix, radix, trigram, or posting-list primitives that allow instant narrowing on mmapped indexes without full-corpus heap scans.
tasks.title: Define compact query primitives for instant source search

phases.title: Define Immutable Source Indexes

exit_criteria: The runtime holds only compact control state and reusable buffers on the heap.
Inactive package sources no longer bloom Private_Dirty through duplicated corpora.
Interactive search and source switching still feel instant.
phases.goal: Move the interactive runtime from heap-owned package databases to compact source views over immutable indexes while keeping source switching instant.

acceptance_criteria: Source switching changes the active mapped view without triggering parse, decompression, or network work.
AppState no longer retains full mutable copies of inactive source corpora.
The interactive path still exposes the same source-selection semantics and package-management actions.
tasks.summary: Refactor AppState so active search uses compact source views over mapped index files instead of keeping systemDB, aurDB, and nimbleDB as duplicated heap-owned corpora.
tasks.title: Replace PackageDB residency with SourceView mappings

acceptance_criteria: Typing cost is bounded by candidate narrowing rather than whole-corpus heap scans.
The query path reuses result and scratch buffers instead of allocating per keystroke.
The implementation keeps source switching and ordinary query updates within the same interactive feel as the current product.
evidence_required: Before and after query timing results
tasks.summary: Replace current package-array filtering with a reusable result-buffer pipeline that narrows against compact index primitives and only touches the mapped sections needed for the active query.
tasks.title: Route search through indexed candidate narrowing

acceptance_criteria: Hot search data no longer contains unnecessary detail strings.
Selected-row detail access is bounded and does not disturb source-switch latency.
The details cache has an explicit size budget justified by measurement.
evidence_required: Measured impact on idle memory and detail latency
tasks.summary: Move descriptions and other long metadata out of the hot path so the selected row can load them through mapped offsets or bounded worker fetches without bloating steady-state heap usage.
tasks.title: Keep package details cold and explicitly budgeted

phases.title: Migrate Runtime To Indexed Source Views

exit_criteria: Idle memory and switching behavior are protected by repeatable regression gates.
The indexed runtime is documented well enough to resist accidental regression to heap-resident corpora.
The project has a durable engineering contract for low-RAM instant search.
phases.goal: Convert the new runtime into a stable engineering contract with enforced memory budgets, latency checks, and documented operational boundaries.

acceptance_criteria: The project has a single documented command that fails when idle memory exceeds the agreed budget.
The gate records the exact binary, workload, and procfs metrics it evaluated.
The budget is measured on the indexed runtime rather than inferred from earlier prototypes.
tasks.summary: Turn the baseline harness into a regression gate that checks idle RSS, PSS, and Private_Dirty against the accepted budget for the indexed runtime.
tasks.title: Enforce idle memory budgets against the new runtime

acceptance_criteria: The harness can drive at least one first aur/ and first nim/ transition and record the observed update timing.
The verification procedure documents what constitutes a visible stall and how output timing is sampled.
The gate is run against the same release build used for memory measurements.
tasks.summary: Add a repeatable switch-latency verification step that proves aur/, nim/, and local switching do not regress into observable stalls under the indexed runtime.
tasks.title: Enforce zero-visible-latency switching behavior

acceptance_criteria: The repository documents index rebuild, validation, and refresh workflows.
The maintenance notes call out the ownership and copy-boundary rules for mapped data.
Future contributors can discover the memory and latency contracts from repo-local documentation.
tasks.summary: Record how indexes are rebuilt, validated, prefaulted, and refreshed so future feature work does not quietly revert the low-RAM design or break the instant-switch product contract.
tasks.title: Document the indexed-runtime maintenance contract

phases.title: Lock In Budgets And Regression Gates

rules.text: The active roadmap is JSON only; markdown roadmap files are historical snapshots or supporting notes.
No task may reintroduce parsing, decompression, network fetches, or other cold-path work into the interactive source-switch path; aur/, nim/, and local switching must remain visually instantaneous.
Memory claims must be supported with repeatable measurements of RSS, PSS, and Private_Dirty from /proc/<pid>/smaps_rollup under the same release build and workload.

When mmap-backed indexes are introduced, the hot path must not materialize Nim heap strings or duplicate mapped buffers unless the reportable validation contract explicitly allows and measures that copy.
Memory work must preserve current search semantics, package visibility, and package-management behavior for local, AUR, and Nimble sources.

title: Parun Active Roadmap
